pages.t: AmbiSecure BioKey — Biometric USB Security Key | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Biometric USB security key with on-device fingerprint sensor. FIDO2 + match-on-device. AAL3-eligible. USB-A or USB-C; NFC variant.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Digital Signature Token — Hardware PKI Token | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Hardware PKI signature token. On-token RSA/ECC, PKCS#11, X.509. eIDAS-compatible flows. Pilot batches from 50 units.

pages.t: AmbiSecure eSIM Solution — SGP.22 / SGP.32 eUICC + OpenID Connect | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Ambimat eSIM platform: SGP.22 consumer, SGP.32 M2M, automotive eUICC, with optional OpenID Connect applet for end-user identity verification.

pages.t: AmbiSecure FIDO2 Nano-Card Applet — embedded secure-element authenticator
pages.d: FIDO2 / CTAP2 applet on a CC EAL5+ secure element, available in a convenient nano-card (4FF) form factor and an MFF2 solderable module for embedded integration. Hardware-backed credentials, ECC P-256, ISO/IEC 7816…

pages.t: AmbiSecure IoT Security Applets — Secure-Element Applets
pages.d: JavaCard applets for IoT device identity. Provisioning, attestation, signed firmware update, mutual TLS credential storage. CC EAL5+ secure element.

pages.t: AmbiSEC Module — IoT Security Co-Processor | Dual-Domain Hardware-Rooted IoT Security | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Dual-domain IoT security co-processor. MCU domain runs the application; an isolated cryptographic security domain holds keys, signs / verifies / encrypts, prevents replay, and authenticates secure OTA. TEC 31318 coverage. Nano-card and MFF2 solderable form factors.

pages.t: AmbiSecure IoT Solution — End-to-End IoT Security Platform | AmbiSecure
pages.d: End-to-end IoT security: secure-element chipset, key manager service, FIDO-enabled enterprise MFA, attestation pipeline. OEM-friendly.

pages.t: AmbiSecure JavaCard Applet Platform — FIDO, PIV, OpenPGP, NDEF | AmbiSecure
pages.d: JavaCard applet platform: FIDO, PIV, OpenPGP, Door Access, Certificate Storage, NDEF, OpenID Connect, SCP02, biometric variants, IoT Security. Multi-applet on one chip.

pages.t: AmbiSecure OnePass Bio Card — Biometric FIDO2 Smart Card | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Biometric FIDO2 smart card with on-card fingerprint match. Template never leaves silicon. No PIN, no reader, no shared secret. Phishing-resistant.

pages.t: AmbiSecure OnePass Card — FIDO2 smart card | AmbiSecure
pages.d: The AmbiSecure OnePass Card is a FIDO2 smart card with resident credentials, NFC + USB, brandable form factor — employee ID and authenticator in one card.

pages.t: AmbiSecure OnePass USB Key — FIDO2 hardware security key | AmbiSecure
pages.d: The AmbiSecure OnePass USB Key is a FIDO-certified hardware security key — USB-A or USB-C, no battery, optional PIV applet. Phishing-resistant enterprise authentication.

pages.t: AmbiSecure PIV Bio Card — PIV with on-card fingerprint match
pages.d: PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on a biometric smart card with on-card fingerprint sensor. Three-factor authentication (have / know / are) on a single ID-1 surface. CC EAL5+ secure element.

pages.t: AmbiSecure PIV Nano-Card Applet — PIV applet on a nano-card secure element
pages.d: PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on a CC EAL5+ secure element, available in convenient nano-card (4FF) and solderable MFF2 packages for OEM embedded integration. Four certificate slots, RSA + ECC, hardware-backed…

pages.t: AmbiSecure PIV Smart Card — PIV applet on a contact + contactless ID-1 card
pages.d: PIV-compatible JavaCard applet on an ISO/IEC 7810 ID-1 smart card with contact + contactless interface. Four certificate slots, RSA + ECC, Windows / macOS smart-card logon, PKCS#11 + minidriver. FIPS 201 functional…

pages.t: AmbiSecure PIV USB Key — PIV applet in a USB-A / USB-C key
pages.d: PIV-compatible JavaCard applet in a USB-A / USB-C key form factor. CCID + WebAuthn dual interface, smart-card logon, PKCS#11, SSH agent forwarding, optional PIN gate. CC EAL5+ secure element.

pages.t: AmbiSecure PKCS Signature Suite — Token + Middleware
pages.d: Hardware signature token with PKCS#11 / PKCS#15 middleware. Cross-platform integration: Acrobat, Office, Mozilla, OpenSC, Windows BaseCSP.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Secure Mail Suite — Email + Document Signing
pages.d: White-label platform for S/MIME email signing, email encryption, PDF/PKCS#7 document signing. Hardware-backed via smart card, PIV, or PKCS#11 token.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Tappable — NFC FIDO2 Authenticator | AmbiSecure
pages.d: NFC-only FIDO2 authenticator in card, keyfob, and sticker form factors. Mobile-first authentication and contactless physical access.
OnePass is a unified hardware-backed identity platform — smart card, USB key, validation server, lifecycle. Built for enterprises that need device-bound rather than syncable identity.
AmbiSecure product catalogue: FIDO2 OnePass smart cards, biometric cards, USB keys, NFC tappable, IoT security chipset, JavaCard applets, digital signature tokens.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Bio Enrollment App — Match-on-Card Biometric Enrollment | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Companion app for AmbiSecure biometric authenticators. Match-on-card / match-on-device enrollment. ISO 19794-2 templates, multi-finger, audit-ready.

pages.t: AmbiSecure ePassport Platform Engineering — Backend + PKI
pages.d: End-to-end ePassport platform engineering. Backend, frontend, PKI (CSCA/DSC/PKD), enrolment, personalisation. ICAO 9303-aligned design.

pages.t: AmbiSecure FIDO Validation Server — Multi-tenant FIDO2 SaaS
pages.d: FIDO-based passwordless authentication platform. Multi-tenant SaaS. JS API + REST. WebAuthn, CTAP2, MDS lookup. Per-tenant policy and attestation verification.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Multi-Card Applet Loading Tool — SCP03 CAP Loader | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Factory tool that loads CAP files onto a stack of smart cards over GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 SCP03. Multi-reader, HSM-backed issuer keys, audit log.

pages.t: AmbiSecure NDEF Personalisation Tool — NFC Forum NDEF Programming | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Programs NDEF URL, vCard, or custom records onto AmbiSecure card and token surfaces. NFC Forum Type 4, bulk programming, audit log.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Security Key Manager — Enterprise Key Lifecycle Tool | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Administrator-facing tool for AmbiSecure key inventory, lifecycle, and audit. Set/Get Master Key, Generate Key Pair, Sign data, audit log, REST API.

pages.t: FIDO Validation Server demo — access posture
pages.d: FIDO Validation Server demo access is request-based — no public tenant signup, no open SaaS preview. Schedule a guided evaluation with engineering.
Full-cycle JavaCard / JCOP applet development. JavaCard 3.0, GlobalPlatform 2.3, JCOP 3.1, PIV, FIDO U2F, FIDO2 CTAP, WebAuthn. CAP files + source delivered.
AmbiSecure engineering services: JavaCard applet development, FIDO Validation Server, tool-chain development (bio enrollment, key manager, applet loader, NDEF personalisation).
Smart-card and FIDO tool-chain development: bio enrollment, security key manager, multi-card applet loader, NDEF personalisation. SCP03 loading, factory MES integration.

pages.t: Card ↔ Reader ↔ SAM — mutual-authentication architecture | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Reference walkthrough of the trust chain that powers closed-loop DESFire deployments — trust zones, key hierarchy, mutual auth flow, offline lifecycle, validator form factors.
CTAP2 is the FIDO Alliance Client-To-Authenticator Protocol — the wire format the browser uses to talk to USB-HID, NFC, and BLE roaming authenticators. Commands, transports, PIN/UV protocols.
How FIDO2 / WebAuthn / CTAP2 work — the trust chain, registration, authentication, attestation, AAGUIDs — and the AmbiSecure products and services that ship it.
FIDO2 is two specs working in concert: WebAuthn (W3C API) and CTAP2 (FIDO Alliance authenticator wire protocol). What's in the family, what each spec does, and how certification levels work.
What JavaCard actually is — bytecode, lifecycle, CAP files, SCP03 loading, persistent vs transient memory — and where it fits in our products and services.

pages.t: MIFARE DESFire — EV1, EV2, EV3 architecture | AmbiSecure
pages.d: What changed across DESFire EV1, EV2, EV3 — application/file model, authentication, secure messaging, transaction MAC. Engineering reference, no exploit content.

Passkeys are WebAuthn credentials with a specific posture. The BE/BS flags decide whether they're device-bound or syncable. This page is the engineering posture page — what to require, what to accept, what to reject.

pages.t: Secure Access Modules — SAM AV2 / AV3 architecture | AmbiSecure
pages.d: Why closed-loop transit and access systems put issuer keys in a Secure Access Module, not in reader firmware. SAM AV2/AV3 capabilities, trust zones, host channel.
What a secure element is, how it differs from TPM and HSM, and where AmbiSecure uses them across cards, USB keys, IoT chipsets, and applet platforms.
The technologies AmbiSecure builds with: JavaCard, Secure Elements, FIDO, NFC/DESFire, PKI, eSIM, APDU, embedded cryptography.
WebAuthn (W3C) — registration and authentication ceremonies, clientDataJSON, authenticatorData, attestation objects, RP ID rules, BE/BS flags. With diagrams and verification checklists.
WebAuthn attestation — fmt formats, attStmt structure, x5c chain validation, FIDO MDS BLOB integration, AAGUID allow-listing, enterprise attestation. The full deployment guide.
Reference for closed-loop fare-collection deployments: DESFire credentials, SAM-protected validators, offline transactions, anti-fraud surface, settlement.
eSIM security — eUICC OS / applets, SM-DP+ integration, GSMA SGP.22 (consumer) / SGP.32 (M2M) compliance, security review across the stack.
Government identity programmes with NIST AAL3 / FIPS 140-3 / CC EAL5+ / eIDAS coverage. Sovereign personalisation, sovereign attestation roots, air-gap-tolerant validation.
JavaCard applet design, implementation, verification, personalisation, and field lifecycle. FIDO2, PIV, OpenPGP, EMV, eID, custom applets at scale.
Offline-authentication architecture — hardware-rooted credential, per-session crypto, deferred reconciliation. Pattern, principles, and where to apply it.
Replace passwords + OTP with hardware-bound FIDO2 credentials — phishing-resistant by construction. Reference architecture, migration path, deployment options.
Real passwordless enterprise — what an end-to-end deployment really looks like. Authenticators, provisioning, recovery, federation, attestation policy, lifecycle automation.
Phishing-resistant MFA has a precise technical meaning — origin-bound, hardware-backed credentials. What WebAuthn buys you, what it doesn't, and how it maps to NIST AAL3 / CISA / OMB M-22-09.
Secure-element integration into your product — chip selection, host driver, JavaCard applet, lifecycle. NXP, Infineon, STMicro families. CC EAL5+ secure elements.
Validator hardware that survives tamper, environmental stress, and unattended deployment. SAM-protected, signed-firmware, field-serviceable.
Physical access control with FIDO2 + DESFire-class credentials. One card opens doors, signs into laptops, badges into elevators.
Smart-card personalisation as a service or in-country line. SCP03 + HSM-backed issuance for FIDO2 / PIV / EMV / eID applets. CC EAL5+ secure elements.
AmbiSecure solutions: passwordless MFA, IoT root of trust, secure provisioning, digital identity, payment security, secure firmware update.
Closed-loop transit-ticketing deployments — DESFire cards, SAM-protected validators, depot edge, back-office settlement.
Workforce identity programme — issuance, daily authentication, recovery, off-boarding. Hardware-rooted with attestation-anchored AAGUID policy and IdP-layer federation.
Combined physical access + passwordless authentication using a single FIDO2 + DESFire-class card. Pilot to deployment in months.
PIV-style smart cards, eID, digital signature tokens, custom JavaCard applets for government and defence deployments. Audit-grade issuance.
Where AmbiSecure ships: banking and payments, government and defence, telecom, healthcare, IoT and industrial, retail and hospitality.
Multi-application DESFire credentials for transit + city services. SAM-based key custody that survives an inter-departmental procurement model.
Closed-loop fare collection, secure validator hardware, SAM-protected key custody for metros, BRT, light rail, ferries, and bus fleets.
Look up FIDO2 / WebAuthn AAGUIDs against a curated offline directory of well-known consumer and enterprise authenticators.
Free, client-side APDU parser. Decode CLA / INS / P1 / P2 / Lc / Le for Case 1-4 (short and extended length), or response APDUs with status word lookup.
Validate a multi-line APDU script line-by-line for ISO 7816-4 case 1, 2, 3, 4 — short and extended length. Validation only; no execution.
Browseable, filterable dictionary of smart-card status words from ISO 7816-4, GlobalPlatform, EMV, and FIDO.
Convert ASCII text to / from HEX. Multiple separator styles, copy-friendly. Client-side only.
Decode any BER/DER-encoded ASN.1 blob into a clickable tree, with X.509 OID lookup. Hex, base64, or PEM input. Client-side.
Walk DER-encoded ASN.1 as a collapsible tree with OID lookup, primitive type recognition, and per-node hex preview. PEM input accepted.
Free, client-side ATR parser. Decode TS, T0, TA/TB/TC/TD interface bytes, historical bytes, and TCK from any smart-card ATR. Nothing leaves your browser.
Decode ISO/IEC 14443-4 ATS bytes — TL, T0, TA(1)/TB(1)/TC(1), historical bytes. Client-side.
Walk WebAuthn authenticatorData — rpIdHash, flags, signCount, AAGUID, credentialId, COSE_Key, extensions.
Detect and decode any certificate blob — PEM, base64, or hex — and emit DER bytes plus a clean PEM. Client-side, multi-block.
Encode or decode base64 (standard and URL-safe variants). Client-side, copy-friendly, sample inputs.
Encode and decode base64url (RFC 4648 §5). The format WebAuthn, JOSE, and CTAP2 use everywhere. Bidirectional conversion — base64url, base64, hex, UTF-8.
Decode and encode BER length octets — short-form, long-form, and indefinite-form. Quick reference for ASN.1 and EMV TLV encoding.
Decode CBOR (RFC 8949) blobs in your browser — integers, byte / text strings, arrays, maps, tags. Friendly for WebAuthn attestation objects, COSE keys, CTAP2 messages.
Visualise a TLS / PKI certificate chain from a multi-cert PEM bundle. Each cert summarised; chain links validated by issuer ↔ subject. Client-side.
Compute SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 fingerprints of an X.509 certificate. Uses Web Crypto. Fully client-side.
Decode a base64url clientDataJSON. Verifies type, extracts challenge / origin / crossOrigin, and computes SHA-256 — what the authenticator actually signs.
Compute CMAC block count, padded length, and output truncation for AES / TDES. Practical companion for SCP03, DESFire EV2/EV3, and EMV CDA implementers.
Inspect COSE_Key blobs (RFC 8152) — kty, alg, crv, x, y, n, e. Decode the credentialPublicKey from any WebAuthn attestation.
Inspect a WebAuthn credentialId — length, entropy, AAGUID-shaped prefix, encoding cross-checks. Diagnostic only; credentialIds are opaque to the RP.
Decode a PKCS#10 Certificate Signing Request — subject, public key, requested extensions, signature algorithm. Client-side only, no upload.
Decode the 16-bit DESFire access-rights word into Read / Write / Read+Write / ChangeAccessRights key references.
Decode the DESFire GetFileSettings response — file type, communication settings, access rights word, per-type fields (Std / Backup / Value / Linear Record / Cyclic Record / TMAC).
Decode the 2-byte DESFire GetKeySettings response — keySettings byte (ChangeKey / config / master-key flags), numKeys, and crypto type (DES / 3DES / AES).
Look up DESFire response status bytes — OPERATION_OK, AUTHENTICATION_ERROR, ADDITIONAL_FRAME, etc. Client-side reference.
Filterable directory of EMV (Books 3, 4, and CL) tags — SELECT, GET PROCESSING OPTIONS, READ RECORD, GENERATE AC, terminal capabilities.
Convert between big-endian (BE) and little-endian (LE) for u16 / u32 / u64 hex blocks. Shows unsigned numeric value too.
Browse the AAGUID directory in metadata-style cards — vendor, model, transports, BE=0 posture. Searchable. Production should fetch the FIDO MDS BLOB; this is an offline stand-in.
Reference for GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 — card lifecycle states, application lifecycle states, ISD permissions.
Convert hex strings into C / Java / Python / JS byte arrays — and back. Multiple programmer formats. Client-side only.
Decode the ISO/IEC 7816-4 CLA byte — interindustry vs proprietary, secure messaging bits, command chaining, logical channel.
Decode ISO/IEC 14443-3 (REQA / WUPA / ATQA / SAK) and 14443-4 (RATS / I-block / R-block / S-block) frame structures.
ISO/IEC 14443 contactless smart cards: parts, Type A vs B, anti-collision, framing, I/R/S blocks. One-page reference.
A bookmark-friendly quick reference card for ISO/IEC 7816 — APDU structure, common INS bytes, file types, security messaging.

Explore JavaCard CAP file components (Header, Directory, Applet, Import, ConstantPool, Class, Method, StaticField, RefLocation, Export, Descriptor, Debug). Decode Header.cap magic / version / package AID.
Educational visualizer for AES-CMAC key diversification (NXP AN10922 / AV2). Shows the SHAPE of the derivation, not a production-grade computation.
Encode an integer length in BER long-form, DGI long-form, 1-byte, and 2-byte BE/LE. Useful for APDU and TLV building.
Decode NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) messages — URI, Text, Smart Poster, MIME, External records.
Step-by-step walkthrough of the WebAuthn registration and authentication ceremonies, with annotation linking to the relevant decoder tools.
Free, client-side PEM ↔ DER converter for certificates, CSRs, public/private keys. PEM block detection, multi-block support, downloadable output.
Inspect the structure of a .pfx / .p12 PKCS#12 archive — bag types, encryption schemes, embedded certificates. Client-side; password-decryption flagged as a current limitation.
Validate WebAuthn rpId / origin combinations. Confirms registrable-suffix rule, HTTPS posture, public-suffix-list constraints.
Educational walkthrough of GlobalPlatform SCP03 (AES-128 mutual authentication and secure messaging). No production key compute — illustration only.
Walk SM-wrapped APDU body data — DOs 87 (cryptogram), 97 (Le), 99 (status), 8E (MAC), 84 (legacy cryptogram). Reference for ISO 7816-4 secure-messaging implementers.
Look up smart-card status words (SW1 SW2) by hex or by phrase. ISO 7816-4, GlobalPlatform, EMV, FIDO common returns.
Free, client-side BER-TLV parser with an EMV tag dictionary. Walks tag/length/value trees of any depth. Nothing leaves your browser.
Visualise BER-TLV / EMV TLV trees as collapsible nested nodes with EMV tag-name lookup. The visual companion to the TLV parser.
Visualise the DESFire EV2/EV3 Transaction MAC field — 4-byte counter (TMC) + 8-byte MAC value (TMV). Educational reference for transit / closed-loop ticketing implementers.
Inspect ISO/IEC 14443 Type A UID — cascade level, manufacturer, fixed vs random.
See exactly how UTF-8 encodes a string — codepoints, byte counts, BOM detection, per-character breakdown.
Decode WebAuthn attestationObject (CBOR with fmt, attStmt, authData). Walks attStmt and links into the authenticatorData parser.
Inspect a WebAuthn challenge — length, Shannon entropy, common pitfalls. Generate fresh 32-byte challenges with the platform CSPRNG.
Decode an X.509 certificate (or chain) into subject, issuer, validity, public key, signature algorithm, and all extensions — fully client-side, no upload.
Searchable APDU status word (SW1/SW2) reference — ISO/IEC 7816-4 standard codes plus GlobalPlatform, EMV, and FIDO U2F-specific values.
Searchable ASN.1 universal-class tag reference. BOOLEAN, INTEGER, OCTET STRING, OBJECT IDENTIFIER, SEQUENCE, SET, and the string types.
Searchable DESFire status code reference — every documented EV1/EV2/EV3 response status with meaning and context.
Searchable EMV tag reference covering Books 3, 4, and Contactless. Filter by category — card-data, transaction, crypto, capability, DOL, template.
Searchable AAGUID directory for FIDO2 / WebAuthn authenticators. Curated subset of well-known consumer and enterprise devices.
Searchable reference for GlobalPlatform 2.3.1 status words across card-management commands (INSTALL, LOAD, DELETE, GET STATUS).
Searchable reference for common ISO/IEC 7816-4 CLA byte values — interindustry, proprietary, GlobalPlatform, secure-messaging variants.
Reference for the 12 standard JavaCard CAP file components — Header, Directory, Applet, Import, ConstantPool, Class, Method, StaticField, ReferenceLocation, Export, Descriptor, Debug.
Reference for NDEF Type Name Format (TNF) values — well-known, MIME, absolute URI, external types, plus the special and reserved values.
Searchable engineering reference databases — APDU status words, EMV tags, ISO 7816 CLA values, COSE algorithms, FIDO AAGUIDs, DESFire status, GlobalPlatform, ASN.1, NDEF, X.509.
Searchable reference for COSE algorithm identifiers used in WebAuthn / FIDO2. ES256, EdDSA, RS256, PS256, and the full IANA registry subset.
WebAuthn engineering reference — registration, authentication, attestation, RP ID rules, BE/BS flags, troubleshooting. Developer-portal layout. Static, fast, copy-able code samples.
Searchable reference for common X.509 / PKIX OIDs — RDN attribute types, certificate extensions, signature algorithms, FIDO attestation extensions.
Reference for common X.509 v3 certificate extensions — SKID, AKID, KU, EKU, SAN, BC, CDP, AIA, certificatePolicies, FIDO AAGUID extension.
A chronological timeline of ePassport standards evolution: ICAO 9303, BAC, PACE, SAC, EAC, multi-application travel documents.
A chronological timeline of FIDO standards evolution, from FIDO 1.0 / U2F through CTAP, FIDO2, WebAuthn, and modern multi-device passkeys.

pages.t: OTP and SMS authentication evolution timeline
pages.d: A chronological timeline of one-time-password authentication standards: S/Key, RSA SecurID, HOTP, TOTP, SMS OTP, and the FIDO-driven phishing-resistance pivot.
A chronological timeline of PIV standards evolution — FIPS 201, SP 800-73, derived PIV credentials, and the modern multi-form-factor PIV deployment.
A chronological timeline of secure-element evolution — from SIM cards through embedded SEs, TPMs, eUICCs, and IoT trust chips.
A chronological timeline of smart-card standards and platform evolution: ISO 7816, JavaCard, GlobalPlatform, DESFire, multi-applet smart cards.
Decade-scale evolution of the standards AmbiSecure builds against — FIDO, PIV, ePassport, OTP, smart cards, WebAuthn, secure elements.
A chronological timeline of WebAuthn and passkey evolution — W3C standardisation, browser support, and the platform shift to synced passkeys.

pages.d: How to keep tap-to-decision under 300 ms while running EV2 mutual auth, transaction MAC, and audit writes. Engineering disciplines that decide whether the deployment ships.

pages.d: A decision-grade comparison of smart-card authenticators, dedicated FIDO security keys, and passkeys. Threat model, lifecycle, recovery, and the choice that fits each deployment.

pages.d: A practical, deployment-focused walkthrough of FIDO2 — CTAP2, WebAuthn, attestation, registration, authentication. Code that compiles, choices that scale.

pages.d: JavaCard applets are how custom enterprise-identity behaviour lives on a smart card. A practical guide to building applets that ship: AID design, lifecycle, secure messaging, personalisation, and the mistakes to avoid.

pages.t: Passkeys vs Traditional MFA — what really changed
pages.d: Passkeys are not MFA-just-stronger. They are a different cryptographic primitive that retires phishing, replay, credential stuffing, and push fatigue as attack classes.

pages.d: Workforce identity credential matrix: PIV smart card vs USB token vs embedded secure element. Lifecycle, physical-logical convergence, certificate workflows, deployment realities.

pages.d: Issuing X.509 credentials at workforce or government scale is more architecture than cryptography. RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit. What gets built and what gets bought.

pages.d: Three classes of hardware key-storage that everyone confuses. What each is actually for, what each refuses to do, and how to choose for device, workstation, or backend.

pages.d: A current, vendor-neutral overview of the services and platforms that accept FIDO2 / WebAuthn authentication — cloud SaaS, OS-level sign-in, IdPs, and developer platforms.

pages.t: Why SAMs Matter in Closed-Loop Transit Systems | AmbiSecure

pages.d: Closed-loop transit ticketing has to keep collecting fares when the backend is unreachable. This is the architecture that lets validators do the right thing without phoning home.
Cybercriminals have billions of leaked credentials. A second factor — especially a hardware-bound one — cuts off the long tail of credential-stuffing and phishing.

pages.t: An Introduction to Java Card Technology (Archive 2020)
pages.d: An introduction to JavaCard — how Java-based applets run securely on smart cards and other small-memory secure elements, why the JavaCard runtime model exists, and what it enables for FIDO, PIV, and ePassport applets.
First of a two-part look at IoT security — how billions of devices communicate with enterprise systems, where the resulting attack surface sits, and the trust anchors that contain it.
Second of a two-part look at IoT security — practical mitigations: hardware root of trust, signed firmware update, attestation, and device identity carried in a secure element.

Unpacks the most common misconceptions about two-factor authentication — what 2FA actually defends against, where SMS-based codes fall short, and why hardware-rooted second factors hold up under credential-theft attacks.

pages.t: Consumer Biometrics in the Data Privacy Age (Archive 2020)
pages.d: How organisations can deploy biometric authentication while still meeting modern privacy expectations — match-on-device versus match-on-server, template storage, and the regulatory shape of consumer biometric data.
Part one of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India and what they reveal about systemic gaps in online identity, authentication, and incident response.
Part two of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — the attack patterns, the identity weaknesses they exploited, and what enterprise authentication posture would have stopped them.
Part three of a three-part analysis of major cyber attacks in India — closing the series with systemic gaps in incident response, identity hygiene, and hardware-rooted authentication adoption.
AmbiSecure engineering archive: FIDO, MFA, JavaCard, smart cards, transit, IoT security pieces from 2017 onwards, with links forward to current coverage.
A field overview of enterprise security threats in the cloud era — how the digital attack surface expands as organisations adopt new platforms, and where hardware-rooted identity narrows it.

pages.t: ePassport and How will chip-based e-Passports work (Archive 2021)
pages.d: How chip-based ePassports work in practice — the BAC / PACE access protocols, the CSCA / DSC / PKD trust chain, and the role of the secure element inside the document.
How biometric passports work in practice — chip-based identity, border-control architecture, and the standards stack that keeps the issuer chain trustworthy.
A primer on FIDO (Fast Identity Online) and the Universal Second Factor model — how FIDO replaces shared secrets with hardware-bound credentials and why it changes the phishing-resistance baseline.
Examines whether passwordless authentication can scale beyond pilots — the standards, attack-surface trade-offs, and operational realities that decide its trajectory.
Why government identity programmes are adopting multi-factor authentication, how MFA holds up against phishing and credential theft, and where hardware-rooted authenticators sit in the stack.
First of three articles on automated fare collection — a comparative look at ticketing architectures across advanced economies and large transit networks in Asia.
Part two of the AFC series — how Ambimat Electronics has approached automated fare collection: validators, SAM-backed offline trust, and revenue assurance.
Part three of the AFC series — comparative notes across the analysed countries, what was common in mature ticketing architectures, and where hardware-rooted secure access modules sit in the stack.
Why industrial IoT infrastructure remains a high-value target — the markets driving adoption, the trust gaps in legacy OT, and how hardware-rooted device identity tightens them.
Compares single sign-on with multi-factor authentication — when each model fits, where they overlap, and how SSO + MFA combine for enterprise identity.

pages.t: SMS-based OTP Authentication and Its Disadvantages (Archive 2021)
pages.d: Why SMS-based one-time passwords are no longer a defensible second factor — SIM-swap attacks, SS7 weaknesses, phishing-prone OTP relay, and the hardware-rooted alternatives.
How governments are moving from password-only access to multi-factor authentication for citizen and employee identity — and where hardware-rooted credentials reshape the threat model.

pages.t: Understanding EMV certification In Public Transportation (Archive 2020)
pages.d: A practical look at EMV certification in public transport — which certifications apply, the roles of acquirer, scheme, terminal vendor, and transit authority, and the typical timeline from prototype to revenue service.
A primer on passwordless authentication — what it actually means, how FIDO and biometrics replace shared secrets, and why hardware-bound credentials sit at the centre of the model.

pages.t: Workplace Security — How Biometrics Is the Key to the New Normal (Archive 2020)
pages.d: How workplace biometrics layer additional security onto existing authentication methods — face / fingerprint / palm, the privacy controls that keep them defensible, and where they sit alongside FIDO and MFA.
Anonymised architecture write-ups from real AmbiSecure deployments — passwordless workforce, transit ticketing, secure device identity.
DESFire EV2 + SAM-backed validator architecture for a metro operator's 700+ gate closed-loop ticketing fleet.
How a 9,000-seat workforce migrated from SMS and TOTP to FIDO2 across Okta, Microsoft Entra ID, and a legacy CAS portal.
Adding hardware-rooted device identity to a connected-device line: secure element on the BOM, SMT-line personalisation, and PKI lifecycle.
A short product hero loop showing the AmbiSecure BioKey biometric USB security key.
A 1m 27s overview of the AmbiSecure smart card across FIDO, PIV, door access, NDEF, and OpenPGP roles — what the multi-application card can carry.

pages.t: AmbiSecure Videos — FIDO setup walkthroughs and product demos
pages.d: AmbiSecure tutorial videos: FIDO U2F setup on Gmail, Facebook, mobile; AmbiSecure card use-case overview; BioKey product loop.

pages.t: How to set up the AmbiSecure card on desktop (U2F / FIDO)
pages.d: A 1m 15s walkthrough of registering the AmbiSecure smart card as a FIDO U2F authenticator on a desktop browser.

pages.t: How to set up the AmbiSecure card on Facebook (U2F / FIDO)
pages.d: Pair a hardware FIDO key with a Facebook account in under 90 seconds. Covers the security key tab under settings, registration, and verification.

pages.t: How to set up the AmbiSecure card on Gmail (U2F / FIDO)
pages.d: Pair the AmbiSecure card with a Google account. Walkthrough of 2-step verification → security key registration on a desktop browser.

pages.t: Set up the AmbiSecure card on your phone — Facebook
pages.d: Quick 24-second clip showing the NFC tap-and-register flow for the AmbiSecure card with the Facebook mobile app.

pages.t: Set up the AmbiSecure card on your phone — Gmail
pages.d: A 1m 15s walkthrough of NFC-tap registration of the AmbiSecure card with a Google account from a mobile device.
Downloadable / printable overviews of each AmbiSecure platform — OnePass, FIDO, transit security, JavaCard, PKI, device identity.
Hardware-rooted device identity for fleet-class IoT — secure element on the BOM, SMT-line personalisation, EST-based rotation.
What an enterprise FIDO2 rollout actually looks like — AAGUID policy, attestation, recovery, common deployment mistakes.
Multi-applet JavaCard platform — FIDO2, PIV, OpenPGP, NDEF, biometric variants, IoT applets, custom enterprise applets, AID-selectable.
OnePass platform overview — smart card, USB key, bio card, validation server, lifecycle. Engineered for hardware-backed identity.
Workforce and government-scale PKI — RA, CA, key custody, attestation, lifecycle, audit. Designed to be defensible.
Closed-loop transit ticketing with DESFire EV2/EV3, SAM-backed validators, offline trust, and sub-300 ms tap-and-go.
AmbiSecure is the security business unit of Ambimat Electronics. Forty years of embedded systems experience, focused on FIDO, JavaCard, Secure Element, and IoT trust.

pages.t: AmbiSecure — Secure Element, FIDO & JavaCard engineering for connected systems
